newsSinceJun6: Goldman Sachs upgraded ROHM to Buy from Neutral on June 8, nearly doubling its price target to JPY 6,500 from JPY 3,300, citing AI-server HVDC voltage migration and ROHM's raised FY30 AI/DC sales target (JPY 100B vs JPY 30B prior, of which SiC JPY 30B) plus an earlier-than-guided SiC breakeven (FY27 vs FY28). Stock followed through +8.87% on June 9 to JPY 5,242 (+130% YTD). Context just before the window: June 3 announcement that ROHM's 750V SiC MOSFET (SCT4013DLL) was adopted in a battery backup unit for AI servers using +/-400V HVDC architecture. Today's move is sell-side-recognition driven, not a new contract: thesis validated, hiddenness reduced.

sources.label: Investing.com - Goldman Sachs upgrades Rohm on AI server growth outlook (PT JPY 6,500 from JPY 3,300)
ROHM press release 2026-06-03 - SiC MOSFET adopted in BBU for AI servers as HVDC architectures advance
Kabushiki Shimbun - ROHM surges on reported expansion of shareholder returns

verdicts.rankRationale: On a residual-alpha, crowding-sensitive list, a headline Goldman upgrade (PT to JPY 6,500) plus an 8.9% follow-through day on top of +130% YTD is a recognition event that consumes the remaining mispricing; no new passives/resistor-specific evidence arrived.
verdicts.bull: GS sees AI/DC sales beating even ROHM's raised JPY 100B FY30 target, and the HVDC migration lifts content per server across power and current-sense parts; PT of JPY 6,500 still sits ~24% above spot.
verdicts.neutral: The AI power thesis is now fully articulated by major sell-side; the stock can grind with estimates but the asymmetric, under-owned setup this page rewards is largely gone at +130% YTD.
verdicts.bear: SiC remains loss-making (~JPY 20B loss expected FY26); any AI-capex digestion or SiC pricing pressure hits a stock that has rerated on a 5-year-out target.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1 FY26 results showing AI/DC order momentum and faster-than-guided SiC loss reduction.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-upgrade crowding unwind; momentum reversal in Japan power-semi names after the GS-driven spike.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the network-centrality lens, the June 3 BBU design win for +/-400V HVDC AI servers and the GS-endorsed JPY 100B FY30 AI/DC target genuinely increase ROHM's directness to the data-center power node, offsetting the rerating; hold after yesterday's down move rather than whipsawing.
verdicts.bull: HVDC architecture adoption makes SiC/power semis a structural AI-server node, and ROHM now has a named design win plus a 3x raised internal AI/DC target.
verdicts.neutral: Directness is improving but ROHM is still a power supplier, not on the HBM/advanced-packaging/CPO critical path this page ranks highest; rank 85 already reflects that.
verdicts.bear: Power for AI servers is a contested node (Infineon, onsemi, Fuji Electric, Mitsubishi Electric); ROHM's edge is unproven at scale and SiC is still in losses.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Further named HVDC/BBU design wins or hyperscaler power-architecture awards confirming node centrality.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Competitors capture the HVDC AI-server power socket, leaving ROHM peripheral to the bottleneck map.

verdicts.rankRationale: Nothing since June 6 makes ROHM CPO-relevant - the GS upgrade is about AI-server power/SiC - and the +8.9% spike on +130% YTD further worsens asymmetry on a CPO-focused actionable list; this reaffirms yesterday's removal call.
verdicts.bull: If the page's scope flexes toward AI data-center power, ROHM's HVDC BBU win and raised AI/DC target are real bottleneck-adjacent evidence.
verdicts.neutral: ROHM is a power-semi story; its rerating says nothing about co-packaged optics supply chains either way.
verdicts.bear: Holding a non-CPO name at rank 96 after a sell-side-driven 9% pop dilutes the list; SiC pricing and the auto cycle remain the dominant earnings drivers.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: None on the CPO axis; only a scope change to this page would change the call.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued drift on a page where the company has no thesis fit.

verdicts.rankRationale: The board-level power thesis is now consensus: a doubled Goldman target, an 8.9% day and +130% YTD mean the scarce, unrecognized bottleneck exposure this alpha score rewards has been priced; the genuinely new information (GS upgrade) justifies moving again after yesterday's down.
verdicts.bull: AI-server PDN content is rising with the HVDC shift, ROHM has a named BBU SiC win, and GS models SiC breakeven a year ahead of guidance - fundamentals are improving, not just the multiple.
verdicts.neutral: Real power-delivery exposure, fully flagged by the street; expect performance to track estimate revisions rather than rerating from here.
verdicts.bear: FY26 operating profit guidance is thin (~JPY 5B on JPY 460B sales) and SiC still burns ~JPY 20B; a 130% YTD stock priced off FY30 targets has little cushion for execution slips.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Quarterly evidence that AI/DC revenue is tracking ahead of the JPY 100B FY30 path.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Valuation air pocket if AI-server power orders pause or SiC losses fail to halve as Goldman models.
